70	func NoopPendingRefExists(_ hash.Hash) bool { return false }
71	
72	// ChunkStore is the core storage abstraction in noms. We can put data
73	// anyplace we have a ChunkStore implementation for.
74	type ChunkStore interface {
75		// Get the Chunk for the value of the hash in the store. If the hash is
76		// absent from the store EmptyChunk is returned.
77		Get(ctx context.Context, h hash.Hash) (Chunk, error)
78	
79		// GetMany gets the Chunks with |hashes| from the store. On return,
80		// |foundChunks| will have been fully sent all chunks which have been
81		// found. Any non-present chunks will silently be ignored.
82		GetMany(ctx context.Context, hashes hash.HashSet, found func(context.Context, *Chunk)) error
83	
84		// Returns true iff the value at the address |h| is contained in the
85		// store
86		Has(ctx context.Context, h hash.Hash) (bool, error)
87	
88		// Returns a new HashSet containing any members of |hashes| that are
89		// absent from the store.
90		HasMany(ctx context.Context, hashes hash.HashSet) (absent hash.HashSet, err error)
91	
92		// Put caches c in the ChunkSource. Upon return, c must be visible to
93		// subsequent Get and Has calls, but must not be persistent until a call
94		// to Flush(). Put may be called concurrently with other calls to Put(),
95		// Get(), GetMany(), Has() and HasMany(). Will return an error if the
96		// addrs returned by `getAddrs` are absent from the chunk store.
97		Put(ctx context.Context, c Chunk, getAddrs InsertAddrsCurry) error
98	
99		// Returns the NomsBinFormat with which this ChunkSource is compatible.
100		Version() string
101	
102		// Returns the current access mode for this opened ChunkStore.
103		AccessMode() ExclusiveAccessMode
104	
105		// Rebase brings this ChunkStore into sync with the persistent storage's
106		// current root.
107		Rebase(ctx context.Context) error
108	
109		// Root returns the root of the database as of the time the ChunkStore
110		// was opened or the most recent call to Rebase.
111		Root(ctx context.Context) (hash.Hash, error)
112	
113		// Commit atomically attempts to persist all novel Chunks and update the
114		// persisted root hash from last to current (or keeps it the same).
115		// If last doesn't match the root in persistent storage, returns false.
116		Commit(ctx context.Context, current, last hash.Hash) (bool, error)
117	
118		// Stats may return some kind of struct that reports statistics about the
119		// ChunkStore instance. The type is implementation-dependent, and impls
120		// may return nil
121		Stats() interface{}
122	
123		// StatsSummary may return a string containing summarized statistics for
124		// this ChunkStore. It must return "Unsupported" if this operation is not
125		// supported.
126		StatsSummary() string
127	
128		// PersistGhostHashes is used to persist a set of addresses that are known to exist, but
129		// are not currently stored here. Only the GenerationalChunkStore implementation allows use of this method, as
130		// shallow clones are only allowed in local copies currently. Note that at the application level, the only
131		// hashes which can be ghosted are commit ids, but the chunk store doesn't know what those are.
132		PersistGhostHashes(ctx context.Context, refs hash.HashSet) error
133	
134		// Close tears down any resources in use by the implementation. After
135		// Close(), the ChunkStore may not be used again. It is NOT SAFE to call
136		// Close() concurrently with any other ChunkStore method; behavior is
137		// undefined and probably crashy.
138		io.Closer

29	type LateBindQueryistResult struct {
30		Queryist Queryist
31		Context  *sql.Context
32		IsRemote bool
33		Closer   func()
34	}
35	
36	type LateBindQueryistConfig struct {
37		EnableAutoGC bool
38	}
39	
40	type LateBindQueryistOption func(*LateBindQueryistConfig)
41	
42	// LateBindQueryist is a function that will be called the first time Queryist is needed for use. Input is a context which
43	// is appropriate for the call to commence. Output is a LateBindQueryistResult, which includes a Queryist, a sql.Context, and
44	// a closer function. It can also result in an error.
45	//
46	// The Closer function should be called when the Queryist is no longer needed. If the result is cached and returned to
47	// multiple callers, it should be called after the cached result itself is no longer needed.
48	//
49	// A LateBindqueryistResult includes enough information for a caller to know if it is connecting to a remote Dolt instance
50	// or if it running the SqlEngine locally in-process. The CliContext uses this, in addition to its own local state, to
51	// let a caller know if they are connected to a remote and if this is the first QueryEngine fetch of the process lifecycle.
52	// This is reflected in |IsRemote| and, in the case of QueryEngineResult, |IsFirstUse|.
53	//
54	// This state is useful for determining whether a command making use of the CliContext is being run within the context of
55	// another command. This is particularly interesting when running a \checkout in a dolt sql session. It makes sense to do
56	// so in the context of `dolt sql`, but not in the context of `dolt checkout` when connected to a remote server.
57	type LateBindQueryist func(ctx context.Context, opts ...LateBindQueryistOption) (LateBindQueryistResult, error)
58	
59	// CliContexct is used to pass top level command information down to subcommands.
60	type CliContext interface {
61		// GlobalArgs returns the arguments passed before the subcommand.
62		GlobalArgs() *argparser.ArgParseResults
63		WorkingDir() filesys.Filesys
64		Config() *env.DoltCliConfig
65		QueryEngine(ctx context.Context, opts ...LateBindQueryistOption) (QueryEngineResult, error)
66		// Release resources associated with the CliContext, including
67		// any QueryEngines which were provisioned over the lifetime
68		// of the CliContext.
69		Close()
70	}
71	
72	// NewCliContext creates a new CliContext instance. Arguments must not be nil.
73	func NewCliContext(args *argparser.ArgParseResults, config *env.DoltCliConfig, cwd filesys.Filesys, latebind LateBindQueryist) (CliContext, errhand.VerboseError) {
74		if args == nil || config == nil || cwd == nil || latebind == nil {
75			return nil, errhand.VerboseErrorFromError(errors.New("Invariant violated. args, config, cwd, and latebind must be non nil."))
76		}
77	
78		return LateBindCliContext{
79			globalArgs:    args,
80			config:        config,
81			cwd:           cwd,
82			activeContext: &QueryistContext{},
83			bind:          latebind,
84		}, nil
85	}
86	
87	type QueryistContext struct {
88		sqlCtx   *sql.Context
89		qryist   *Queryist
90		isRemote bool
91		close    func()
92	}
93	
94	// LateBindCliContext is a struct that implements CliContext. Its primary purpose is to wrap the global arguments and
95	// provide an implementation of the QueryEngine function. This instance is stateful to ensure that the Queryist is only
96	// created once.
97	type LateBindCliContext struct {
98		globalArgs    *argparser.ArgParseResults
99		cwd           filesys.Filesys
100		config        *env.DoltCliConfig
101		activeContext *QueryistContext
102	
103		bind LateBindQueryist
104	}
105	
106	type QueryEngineResult struct {
107		Queryist Queryist
108		Context  *sql.Context
109		// |true| if this is the first time the CliContext is returning a QueryEngineResult.
110		// Otherwise it will be |false|, which means this CliContext has already been used
111		// to retrieve a Queryist, and the Queryist coming back is the cached result.
112		IsFirstResult bool
113		IsRemote      bool
114	}
115	
116	// GlobalArgs returns the arguments passed before the subcommand.
117	func (lbc LateBindCliContext) GlobalArgs() *argparser.ArgParseResults {
118		return lbc.globalArgs
119	}
120	
121	// QueryEngine returns a Queryist, a sql.Context, a closer function, and an error. It ensures that only one call to the
122	// LateBindQueryist is made, and caches the result. Note that if this is called twice, the closer function returns will
123	// be nil, callers should check if is nil.
124	func (lbc LateBindCliContext) QueryEngine(ctx context.Context, opts ...LateBindQueryistOption) (res QueryEngineResult, err error) {
125		if lbc.activeContext != nil && lbc.activeContext.qryist != nil && lbc.activeContext.sqlCtx != nil {
126			res.Queryist = *lbc.activeContext.qryist
127			res.Context = lbc.activeContext.sqlCtx
128			res.IsRemote = lbc.activeContext.isRemote
129			// Returning a cached result.
130			res.IsFirstResult = false
131			return res, nil
132		}
133	
134		bindRes, err := lbc.bind(ctx, opts...)
135		if err != nil {
136			return res, err
137		}
138	
139		lbc.activeContext.qryist = &bindRes.Queryist
140		lbc.activeContext.sqlCtx = bindRes.Context
141		lbc.activeContext.close = bindRes.Closer
142		lbc.activeContext.isRemote = bindRes.IsRemote
143	
144		res.Queryist = bindRes.Queryist
145		res.Context = bindRes.Context
146		res.IsRemote = bindRes.IsRemote
147		res.IsFirstResult = true
148		return res, nil
149	}
150	
151	func (lbc LateBindCliContext) Close() {
152		if lbc.activeContext != nil && lbc.activeContext.close != nil {
153			lbc.activeContext.close()
154		}
155	}
156	
157	func (lbc LateBindCliContext) WorkingDir() filesys.Filesys {
158		return lbc.cwd
159	}
160	
161	// Config returns the dolt config stored in CliContext
162	func (lbc LateBindCliContext) Config() *env.DoltCliConfig {
163		return lbc.config
164	}
165	
166	var _ CliContext = LateBindCliContext{}

234	func newLateBindingEngine(
235		cfgDirPath string,
236		privsFp string,
237		branchControlFilePath string,
238		creds *cli.UserPassword,
239		database string,
240		mrEnv *env.MultiRepoEnv,
241	) (cli.LateBindQueryist, error) {
242	
243		config := &engine.SqlEngineConfig{
244			DoltCfgDirPath:     cfgDirPath,
245			PrivFilePath:       privsFp,
246			BranchCtrlFilePath: branchControlFilePath,
247			ServerUser:         creds.Username,
248			ServerPass:         creds.Password,
249			ServerHost:         "localhost",
250			Autocommit:         true,
251		}
252	
253		var lateBinder cli.LateBindQueryist = func(ctx context.Context, opts ...cli.LateBindQueryistOption) (res cli.LateBindQueryistResult, err error) {
254			// We've deferred loading the database as long as we can.
255			// If we're binding the Queryist, that means that engine is actually
256			// going to be used.
257			mrEnv.ReloadDBs(ctx)
258	
259			queryistConfig := &cli.LateBindQueryistConfig{}
260			for _, opt := range opts {
261				opt(queryistConfig)
262			}
263	
264			gcSch := os.Getenv("DOLT_GC_SCHEDULER")
265			if queryistConfig.EnableAutoGC {
266				// We use a null logger here, as we do not want `dolt sql` output
267				// to include auto-gc log lines.
268				nullLgr := logrus.New()
269				nullLgr.SetOutput(io.Discard)
270				config.AutoGCController = sqle.NewAutoGCController(chunks.SimpleArchive, chunks.IncrementalGCTablesDisabled, sqle.NewGCScheduler(gcSch), nullLgr)
271			}
272	
273			se, err := engine.NewSqlEngine(
274				ctx,
275				mrEnv,
276				config,
277			)
278			if err != nil {
279				return res, err
280			}
281	
282			if err := se.InitStats(ctx); err != nil {
283				se.Close()
284				return res, err
285			}
286	
287			rawDb := se.GetUnderlyingEngine().Analyzer.Catalog.MySQLDb
288			salt, err := mysql.NewSalt()
289			if err != nil {
290				se.Close()
291				return res, err
292			}
293	
294			var dbUser string
295			if creds.Specified {
296				dbUser = creds.Username
297	
298				// When running in local mode, we want to attempt respect the user/pwd they provided. If they didn't provide
299				// one, we'll give then super user privs. Respecting the user/pwd is not a security stance - it's there
300				// to enable testing of application settings.
301	
302				authResponse := buildAuthResponse(salt, config.ServerPass)
303	
304				err := passwordValidate(rawDb, salt, dbUser, authResponse)
305				if err != nil {
306					se.Close()
307					return res, err
308				}
309	
310			} else {
311				// Ensure a root user exists, with superuser privs
312				dbUser = DefaultUser
313				ed := rawDb.Editor()
314				rawDb.AddEphemeralSuperUser(ed, dbUser, config.ServerHost, "")
315				ed.Close()
316			}
317	
318			sqlCtx, err := se.NewDefaultContext(ctx)
319			if err != nil {
320				se.Close()
321				return res, err
322			}
323			// Whether we're running in shell mode or some other mode, sql commands from the command line always have a current
324			// database set when you begin using them.
325			sqlCtx.SetCurrentDatabase(database)
326	
327			// SetClient must come before InitClientCommitIdentSession since it requires an authenticated client on the context.
328			sqlCtx.Session.SetClient(sql.Client{User: dbUser, Address: config.ServerHost, Capabilities: 0})
329	
330			// For now, we treat the entire lifecycle of this
331			// sqlCtx as one big session-in-use window.

content.text:
I have the full picture. Here is the audit.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Dolt** is a SQL database with Git-style versioning (fork/clone/branch/merge/push/pull). Go, ~1,560 files / 22,660 symbols.

Layout:
- `go/cmd/dolt/` — CLI entry (`dolt.go`), commands (`commands/`), the CLI↔engine bridge (`commands/engine/sqlengine.go`), server (`commands/sqlserver/`).
- `go/libraries/doltcore/` — the domain. `doltdb/` (**the core object**), `env/` (repo state / DoltEnv), `sqle/` (go-mysql-server integration: databases, provider, sessions in `dsess/`, system tables in `dtables/`, cluster replication in `cluster/`, stats in `statspro/`), `dbfactory/` (opens storage by URL).
- `go/store/` — the Noms-derived storage engine: `chunks/` (ChunkStore abstraction), `types/` (ValueStore), `prolly/tree/` (NodeStore), `datas/` (Database/dataset layer), `nbs/` (Noms Block Store, on-disk).

**The core object**: `doltdb.DoltDB` — `go/libraries/doltcore/doltdb/doltdb.go:126`
```go
type DoltDB struct {
    db  hooksDatabase           // datas.Database + commit hooks
    vrw types.ValueReadWriter
    ns  tree.NodeStore
    databaseName string
    commitCache  *lru.Cache[hash.Hash, *OptionalCommit]
}
```
Constructed at `doltdb.go:148` (`DoltDBFromCS`) and `doltdb.go:187` (`LoadDoltDBWithParams` → `dbfactory.CreateDB`). Its storage layers: `doltdb.hooksDatabase` (`hooksdatabase.go:26`) → `datas.database` (`go/store/datas/database_common.go:37`) → `types.ValueStore` (`go/store/types/value_store.go:75`) / `tree.nodeStore` (`go/store/prolly/tree/node_store.go:65`) → `chunks.ChunkStore` (`go/store/chunks/chunk_store.go:74`) → `nbs.GenerationalNBS` (`go/store/nbs/generational_chunk_store.go:40`) / `nbs.NomsBlockStore` (`go/store/nbs/store.go:96`).

---

## Step 2 — The storage-lifecycle contract

| Layer | file:line | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `DoltDB.NomsRoot` | `doltdb/doltdb.go:239` | read current storage root: `datas.ChunkStoreFromDatabase(ddb.db).Root(ctx)` |
| `DoltDB.CommitRoot` | `doltdb/doltdb.go:248` | **the root swap** — CAS `Commit(ctx, current, last)` on the chunk store |
| `DoltDB.AccessMode` / `.Has` | `doltdb.go:243`, `doltdb.go:252` | other direct reach-throughs to the chunk store |
| `doltdb.hooksDatabase` | `doltdb/hooksdatabase.go:26` | embeds `datas.Database`, back-pointer `db *DoltDB`, fires `hooks []CommitHook` after each successful root swap |
| `datas.database` | `store/datas/database_common.go:37` | dataset/commit layer; embeds `*types.ValueStore`, holds `rt rootTracker`, `ns tree.NodeStore` |
| `datas.rootTracker` | `store/datas/database_common.go:51` | **the narrowed root contract** — just `Root()` + `Commit()`; satisfied by `*types.ValueStore` (`database_common.go:67`), wired at `newDatabase` `database_common.go:56-61` |
| `datas.ChunkStoreFromDatabase` | `store/datas/database.go:221` | the escape hatch DoltDB uses to reach chunk storage |
| `types.ValueStore` | `store/types/value_store.go:75` | value read/write + GC state (`gcState`, `gcNewAddrs`); field `cs chunks.ChunkStore` |
| `tree.nodeStore` | `store/prolly/tree/node_store.go:65` | prolly node read/write; field `store chunks.ChunkStore` + node cache |
| `chunks.ChunkStore` | `store/chunks/chunk_store.go:74` | the storage interface; `Rebase` :107, `Root` :111, `Commit` :116 |
| `nbs.GenerationalNBS` | `store/nbs/generational_chunk_store.go:40` | old/new/ghost generation split; `Root` :307 |
| `nbs.NomsBlockStore` | `store/nbs/store.go:96` | manifest + tableSet + memtable; `upstream manifestContents` :121 is the cached root; `Root` :1548 |

Contract in one line: a query mutates in memory → `ValueStore` writes chunks → `DoltDB.CommitRoot` CAS-swaps the manifest root → `hooksDatabase` fires `CommitHook`s. `Rebase` is how a store picks up someone else's swap.

## Step 3 — How query access is handed out

1. `main.buildLateBinder` — `go/cmd/dolt/dolt.go:636` — CLI decides local vs remote.
2. `commands.newLateBindingEngine` — `go/cmd/dolt/commands/utils.go:234` — builds `engine.SqlEngineConfig` (`utils.go:243`) and returns a `cli.LateBindQueryist` closure (`utils.go:253`).
3. Closure calls `mrEnv.ReloadDBs(ctx)` (`utils.go:257`) then `engine.NewSqlEngine(ctx, mrEnv, config)` (`utils.go:273`).
4. `engine.NewSqlEngine` — `go/cmd/dolt/commands/engine/sqlengine.go:109` → `CollectDBs(ctx, mrEnv)` `sqlengine.go:153` turns each `env.DoltEnv` (holding `doltDB`) into `dsess.SqlDatabase` (i.e. `sqle.Database` with its `ddb` field).

6. `SqlEngine` **is** the `Queryist` (`cli.Queryist`, `go/cmd/dolt/cli/command.go:103`; satisfied at `sqlengine.go:59`).
7. Handover: `LateBindQueryistResult.Queryist` — `go/cmd/dolt/cli/cli_context.go:30`.
8. `LateBindCliContext.QueryEngine` — `cli_context.go:124` — calls `lbc.bind(ctx, opts...)` at :134 and **caches** into `lbc.activeContext.qryist` at :139 (plus `sqlCtx` :140, `close` :141).
9. Retention point: `cli.QueryistContext.qryist *Queryist` — `go/cmd/dolt/cli/cli_context.go:89`, held by `LateBindCliContext.activeContext` — `cli_context.go:101`. Released only via `LateBindCliContext.Close()` — `cli_context.go:151`.
10. Downstream commands then retain it themselves, e.g. `commands.patchState.queryist` — `go/cmd/dolt/commands/add.go:406`.
11. Session side: `engine.sessionFactory` (`sqlengine.go:62,67`) builds `dsess.DoltSession` (`go/libraries/doltcore/sqle/dsess/session.go:48`), whose `provider` :49 and `dbStates` :65 → `DatabaseSessionState.heads` (`database_session_state.go:69`) → `branchState.dbData env.DbData` (`database_session_state.go:104`) → `env.DbData.Ddb *doltdb.DoltDB` (`go/libraries/doltcore/env/repo_state.go:59`).

## Step 6 — Blast radius, ranked

I have the full picture. Here is the audit.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Dolt** is a SQL database with Git-style versioning (fork/clone/branch/merge/push/pull). Go, ~1,560 files / 22,660 symbols.

Layout:
- `go/cmd/dolt/` — CLI entry (`dolt.go`), commands (`commands/`), the CLI↔engine bridge (`commands/engine/sqlengine.go`), server (`commands/sqlserver/`).
- `go/libraries/doltcore/` — the domain. `doltdb/` (**the core object**), `env/` (repo state / DoltEnv), `sqle/` (go-mysql-server integration: databases, provider, sessions in `dsess/`, system tables in `dtables/`, cluster replication in `cluster/`, stats in `statspro/`), `dbfactory/` (opens storage by URL).
- `go/store/` — the Noms-derived storage engine: `chunks/` (ChunkStore abstraction), `types/` (ValueStore), `prolly/tree/` (NodeStore), `datas/` (Database/dataset layer), `nbs/` (Noms Block Store, on-disk).

**The core object**: `doltdb.DoltDB` — `go/libraries/doltcore/doltdb/doltdb.go:126`
```go
type DoltDB struct {
    db  hooksDatabase           // datas.Database + commit hooks
    vrw types.ValueReadWriter
    ns  tree.NodeStore
    databaseName string
    commitCache  *lru.Cache[hash.Hash, *OptionalCommit]
}
```
Constructed at `doltdb.go:148` (`DoltDBFromCS`) and `doltdb.go:187` (`LoadDoltDBWithParams` → `dbfactory.CreateDB`). Its storage layers: `doltdb.hooksDatabase` (`hooksdatabase.go:26`) → `datas.database` (`go/store/datas/database_common.go:37`) → `types.ValueStore` (`go/store/types/value_store.go:75`) / `tree.nodeStore` (`go/store/prolly/tree/node_store.go:65`) → `chunks.ChunkStore` (`go/store/chunks/chunk_store.go:74`) → `nbs.GenerationalNBS` (`go/store/nbs/generational_chunk_store.go:40`) / `nbs.NomsBlockStore` (`go/store/nbs/store.go:96`).

## Step 2 — The storage-lifecycle contract

| Layer | file:line | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `DoltDB.NomsRoot` | `doltdb/doltdb.go:239` | read current storage root: `datas.ChunkStoreFromDatabase(ddb.db).Root(ctx)` |
| `DoltDB.CommitRoot` | `doltdb/doltdb.go:248` | **the root swap** — CAS `Commit(ctx, current, last)` on the chunk store |
| `DoltDB.AccessMode` / `.Has` | `doltdb.go:243`, `doltdb.go:252` | other direct reach-throughs to the chunk store |
| `doltdb.hooksDatabase` | `doltdb/hooksdatabase.go:26` | embeds `datas.Database`, back-pointer `db *DoltDB`, fires `hooks []CommitHook` after each successful root swap |
| `datas.database` | `store/datas/database_common.go:37` | dataset/commit layer; embeds `*types.ValueStore`, holds `rt rootTracker`, `ns tree.NodeStore` |
| `datas.rootTracker` | `store/datas/database_common.go:51` | **the narrowed root contract** — just `Root()` + `Commit()`; satisfied by `*types.ValueStore` (`database_common.go:67`), wired at `newDatabase` `database_common.go:56-61` |
| `datas.ChunkStoreFromDatabase` | `store/datas/database.go:221` | the escape hatch DoltDB uses to reach chunk storage |
| `types.ValueStore` | `store/types/value_store.go:75` | value read/write + GC state (`gcState`, `gcNewAddrs`); field `cs chunks.ChunkStore` |
| `tree.nodeStore` | `store/prolly/tree/node_store.go:65` | prolly node read/write; field `store chunks.ChunkStore` + node cache |
| `chunks.ChunkStore` | `store/chunks/chunk_store.go:74` | the storage interface; `Rebase` :107, `Root` :111, `Commit` :116 |
| `nbs.GenerationalNBS` | `store/nbs/generational_chunk_store.go:40` | old/new/ghost generation split; `Root` :307 |
| `nbs.NomsBlockStore` | `store/nbs/store.go:96` | manifest + tableSet + memtable; `upstream manifestContents` :121 is the cached root; `Root` :1548 |
| `dbfactory.CreateDB` | via `doltdb.go:217` | opens the concrete chunk store per URL scheme |

Contract in one line: a query mutates in memory → `ValueStore` writes chunks → `DoltDB.CommitRoot` CAS-swaps the manifest root → `hooksDatabase` fires `CommitHook`s. `Rebase` is how a store picks up someone else's swap.

## Step 3 — How query access is handed out

1. `main.buildLateBinder` — `go/cmd/dolt/dolt.go:636` — CLI decides local vs remote.
2. `commands.newLateBindingEngine` — `go/cmd/dolt/commands/utils.go:234` — builds `engine.SqlEngineConfig` (`utils.go:243`) and returns a `cli.LateBindQueryist` closure (`utils.go:253`).
3. Closure calls `mrEnv.ReloadDBs(ctx)` (`utils.go:257`) then `engine.NewSqlEngine(ctx, mrEnv, config)` (`utils.go:273`).
4. `engine.NewSqlEngine` — `go/cmd/dolt/commands/engine/sqlengine.go:109` → `CollectDBs(ctx, mrEnv)` `sqlengine.go:153` turns each `env.DoltEnv` (holding `doltDB`) into `dsess.SqlDatabase` (i.e. `sqle.Database` with its `ddb` field).
5. Databases go into `sqle.DoltDatabaseProvider` (`go/libraries/doltcore/sqle/database_provider.go:55`, field `databases map[string]dsess.SqlDatabase`), retained on `engine.SqlEngine.provider` — `go/cmd/dolt/commands/engine/sqlengine.go:60`.
6. `SqlEngine` **is** the `Queryist` (`cli.Queryist`, `go/cmd/dolt/cli/command.go:103`; satisfied at `sqlengine.go:59`).
7. Handover: `LateBindQueryistResult.Queryist` — `go/cmd/dolt/cli/cli_context.go:30`.
8. `LateBindCliContext.QueryEngine` — `cli_context.go:124` — calls `lbc.bind(ctx, opts...)` at :134 and **caches** into `lbc.activeContext.qryist` at :139 (plus `sqlCtx` :140, `close` :141).
9. Retention point: `cli.QueryistContext.qryist *Queryist` — `go/cmd/dolt/cli/cli_context.go:89`, held by `LateBindCliContext.activeContext` — `cli_context.go:101`. Released only via `LateBindCliContext.Close()` — `cli_context.go:151`.
10. Downstream commands then retain it themselves, e.g. `commands.patchState.queryist` — `go/cmd/dolt/commands/add.go:406`.
11. Session side: `engine.sessionFactory` (`sqlengine.go:62,67`) builds `dsess.DoltSession` (`go/libraries/doltcore/sqle/dsess/session.go:48`), whose `provider` :49 and `dbStates` :65 → `DatabaseSessionState.heads` (`database_session_state.go:69`) → `branchState.dbData env.DbData` (`database_session_state.go:104`) → `env.DbData.Ddb *doltdb.DoltDB` (`go/libraries/doltcore/env/repo_state.go:59`).

## Step 6 — Blast radius, ranked

`sense_blast` on `doltdb.DoltDB`: **risk high**, 106 direct callers, 215 affected symbols across 39 files, 53 interface-mediated retainers, heaviest areas `env/actions` (34), `sqle` (8), `doltdb` (5), `dsess` (4), `dtables` (4).

**Tier 1 — will break or leak silently, no compile-time signal**
1. `dbfactory.singletonDB` `file.go:89` + `singletons` `file.go:96` — process-global cache of `datas.Database`/vrw/ns keyed by path. A `DoltDB` rebuilt with a new storage root will silently reuse the old cached store. `CloseAllLocalDatabases` `file.go:99` and `DeleteFromSingletonCache` `file.go:110` are the only invalidation.
2. `dbfactory.gitRemoteCacheEntry` `git_remote.go:99` + `gitRemoteCache` `git_remote.go:106` — same hazard, plus `TeardownGitRemotes` `git_remote.go:110`.
3. `datas.database.rt` `database_common.go:39` — the root contract itself. Every root read/CAS in the process goes through this two-method interface; changing root tracking changes it and nothing else references `DoltDB`.
4. `main.LocalCSCache.dbs` `cscache.go:32`, `SingletonCSCache.s` `cscache.go:76`, `commands.singletonDBCache.cs` `transfer.go:263` — NBS stores cached for the process lifetime, reached only as `remotesrv.RemoteSrvStore`.
5. `sqle.hooksFiringRemoteSrvStore.Commit` `sqle/remotesrv.go:95` and `cluster.remotesrvStore.Commit` `cluster/remotesrv.go:43` — **the second root-swap path**. If the rework only touches `DoltDB.CommitRoot` `doltdb.go:248`, these diverge.
6. `cli.QueryistContext.qryist` `cli_context.go:89` — a whole `SqlEngine` (and its provider and every `DoltDB`) cached per CLI process; released only at `cli_context.go:151`.

**Tier 2 — likely to break, but typed and visible**
7. `cluster.commithook.srcDB/destDB` `commithook.go:50,52` plus cached roots `lastPushedHead`/`nextHead` `:73,:74` and lazy `destDBF` `:47` — cached root hashes go stale on a root-tracking change; note `errDestDBRootHashMoved` `commithook.go:80`.
8. `sqle.AutoGCController.hooks` `auto_gc.go:96` / `autoGCWork.db` `auto_gc.go:136` / `autoGCCommitHook` `auto_gc.go:278` — GC operates directly on storage generations; interacts with `types.ValueStore.gcState` `value_store.go:80` and `NomsBlockStore.gcCond` `store.go:114`.
9. `gcctx.GCSafepointController.sessions` `gc_safepoint_controller.go:29` + `dsess.DoltSession.gcSafepointController` `session.go:67` — safepoint establishment assumes the current root/session lifecycle.
10. `sqle.PushOnWriteHook.destDb` `commit_hooks.go:37`, `AsyncPushOnWriteHook.destDb` `commit_hooks.go:111` with its buffered `ch` :109, `PushArg` `commit_hooks.go:100` — in-flight pushes hold both DBs across a root swap.
11. `env.DoltEnv.doltDB` `environment.go:90` behind `loadDBOnce` `environment.go:89` — a lazily-loaded singleton that can never be re-loaded; any rework that wants to re-open storage must address this.
12. `statspro.prollyStats.ddb` `stats_kv.go:212` — owns and `Close()`s a `*DoltDB` (`stats_kv.go:222`) independent of the main lifecycle; plus `StatsController.kv` `controller.go:101`.

**Tier 3 — stale-cache correctness, not crashes**
13. `doltdb.DoltDB.commitCache` `doltdb.go:139` — LRU of materialized commits, keyed by hash, not invalidated on root change.
14. `dsess.SessionCache` `session_cache.go:39-55` and `DatabaseSessionState.headCache` `database_session_state.go:73` — explicitly documented as persisting across transactions.
15. `dsess.DatabaseCache.initialDbStates` `session_cache.go:70` — keyed by noms root; correctness depends on root identity semantics.
16. `types.ValueStore.decodedChunks` `value_store.go:77`, `tree.nodeStore.cache` `node_store.go:67`, `NomsBlockStore.hasCache` `store.go:115`, `NomsBlockStore.upstream` `store.go:121` — all cached chunk/root state that must be invalidated on `Rebase`.
17. `commitwalk.doltDBResolver.cache` `commitwalk.go:35` behind `commitgraph.HashResolver` — a per-walk hash cache with no tie to `DoltDB`.

**Tier 4 — mechanical, compiler will find them**
18. All ~40 `dtables.*` / `sqle.*` system tables and iterators holding `*DoltDB` or `dsess.SqlDatabase` (Step 4, area I) — they re-read roots each partition; recompile + smoke test.
19. `env.DbData.Ddb` `repo_state.go:59`, `env.PushOptions` `remotes.go:189`, `dprocedures` safepoint controllers `dolt_gc.go:76,169`.
20. The `env/actions` area — 34 direct callers, the largest single cluster; per-call-site, not per-holder.

**Must be re-verified after the change:** every Tier-1/2 cache invalidation path; both root-swap paths agree; `Rebase` still drops all Tier-3 caches; auto-GC + safepoint under concurrent sessions; cluster standby replication root tracking; CLI `LateBindCliContext.Close()` still releases the engine.
